title: Current UI stabilization baseline is not green
description: The current build passes, but qa:ui, qa:css, qa:architecture, qa:visual, qa:functional, and git diff --check fail in the live audit run.

evidence.note: Current command results collected during this audit.
Visual QA fails when runtime module registry contains a module not listed in visual snapshot module IDs.
Functional smoke expects visualSystem >= 8 panels, current smoke observed 1.

impact: Previous pass claims cannot be used as current proof. Any UI refactor has to start from a failing baseline.
recommendedAction: Separate current baseline failures into explicit repair tasks before treating guardrails as reliable release gates.

title: CSS cascade remains large and high-risk
description: Source CSS has 29154 lines, 3070 !important usages, 1847 raw hex values, 7309 raw px values, and 1300 duplicate selector groups by audit parser.

evidence.note: Current CSS counters.
Top risky CSS file by current audit: high important/raw value pressure.
Second-highest risk CSS file and current weekly/planning/table concentration.

impact: Changing a visual rule globally is still risky because selector families and raw declarations compete across layers.
recommendedAction: Treat CSS debt as budgets per family, not only per file: actions/buttons, tables, shell/sidebar, visualSystem, Gantt.

title: Planning CSS has an exact duplicate rule budget failure
description: qa:css fails because an exact duplicate selector exists in styles/ui/planning-order.css for the fifth planning table column.

evidence.note: First width declaration for fifth column.
Second exact duplicate width declaration.
qa:css failure reason records the duplicate rule.

impact: The table redesign work reintroduced a simple duplicate despite previous duplicate budgets.
recommendedAction: Make duplicate selector budget fail before final reports are accepted, then collapse planning-order duplicate families.

title: Runtime is still dominated by src/app.js
description: src/app.js is 40436 lines with 293 render helper functions in the monolith; only 2 module renderer files are extracted.

evidence.note: Main render switch still owns module routing/render binding.
One small extracted module renderer exists.
Second extracted module renderer exists.

impact: UI changes often require editing the monolith, so local fixes can easily affect unrelated modules.
recommendedAction: Continue runtime decomposition by extracting high-churn UI modules after audit baseline is green.

title: Token layer exists but adoption is partial
description: 295 mes tokens exist and 2875 token references were counted, but 9156 raw visual values remain and multiple density/typography aliases have zero references.

evidence.note: Token registry exists.
Control/density/font aliases with zero references are listed.
Current qa:ui raw-token failure lines use local px/rgba values.

impact: A request like changing spacing or typography globally still reaches only part of the system.
recommendedAction: Move from token declaration to adoption budgets: density, typography, control, table, and icon tokens should have minimum usage and raw-value ceilings.

title: Table contract is present but not universal
description: Audit found 38 raw table tags, 23 of 33 classified tables under TableWrap, and 2448 table-related CSS selectors.

evidence.note: Shared TableWrap helper exists.
Prior table budget reports 33 tables and 23 under TableWrap.
Current raw table and selector counts.

impact: Density, selected row, hover, sticky, and overflow behavior can still differ by module.
recommendedAction: Prioritize planning, shiftWorkOrders, routes/products, timesheet, and productionStructureMatrix table families for one table-density contract.

title: Button contract wraps legacy classes instead of replacing them
description: renderUiActionButton exists, but its base class still emits primary-button/secondary-button/icon-button/table-icon-button, and source/runtime still contain 60 secondary-button and 15 primary-button occurrences.

evidence.note: Shared ActionButton maps tone to legacy button classes.
Contract itself includes legacy class selectors.
Current direct class counts.

impact: Fixing a button bug may still require touching legacy and new selectors together.
recommendedAction: Split semantic button API from legacy compatibility classes and shrink direct primary/secondary usage budget.

title: Icon pipeline is richer than runtime adoption
description: Icon assets include 129 semantic slugs, 47 custom SVGs, 82 open-source mappings and 1 fallback SVG, but runtime still uses a generic icon(name) helper 213 times and runtime mixed registry has 3 entries.

evidence.note: Manifest declares custom/open-source/fallback policy and entries.
Runtime icon rendering is still a generic helper path.
Custom icon registry exists.

impact: Approved icon semantics can drift when runtime calls are still name-based instead of semantic-source validated.
recommendedAction: Audit all icon(name) calls into semantic/system/fallback categories and gate unmapped names.

title: Gantt remains a special runtime outside normal UI primitives
description: Gantt slots, timeline cells, week boundaries and segmented slot geometry are built with inline style geometry and specialized functions, not normal component primitives.

evidence.note: Timeline geometry is inline left/width px.
Slot geometry uses inline left/top/width/height and CSS vars.
Shared GanttBar is a demo/contract sample, not the live Gantt renderer.

impact: Mass CSS changes can break geometry/hit areas; Gantt needs its own contract and visual guardrails.
recommendedAction: Keep Gantt under special-runtime budget and do not migrate geometry through broad CSS cleanup.

title: Visual system page and smoke expectations are out of sync
description: qa:functional fails because the visualSystem smoke expects >=8 panels, but current runtime returns 1. This means UI-state docs/tests are not aligned with the active UI state module.

evidence.note: Smoke expectations for visual system page.
Current failure reason: visualSystem expected panels got 1.
Active visualSystem route delegates to V2 page.

impact: The internal UI-kit reference cannot be trusted as a complete visual acceptance surface until smoke and page match.
recommendedAction: Decide whether visualSystem V2 is intentionally compact, then update smoke or rebuild the sections to the stated contract.

title: FormField adoption is low compared with raw controls
description: renderUiFormField is called 9 times, while raw input/select/textarea tags total 129.

evidence.note: Shared form helper exists.
Current raw control counts.
Example of modal form controls/actions assembled locally.

impact: Control height, label alignment, focus, disabled and validation states remain fragmented.
recommendedAction: Introduce form-control coverage budgets per module and migrate high-touch forms first: auth, timesheet, planning labor, roles.

title: Accessibility coverage is opportunistic, not systematic
description: Helpers include some roles/aria attributes, but raw button/input/modal markup and icon-only actions are still widespread and not all checked by a dedicated accessibility audit.

evidence.note: Button helper has data-ui-component but no required aria-label policy for icon-only labels.
Modal helper sets role/dialog but no focus trap evidence.
No lint/a11y script is available in current package scripts.

impact: Keyboard and screen-reader regressions can pass current QA.
recommendedAction: Add non-invasive a11y audit coverage: focusable controls, icon-button labels, modal focus, form labels, contrast token checks.

title: Responsive QA exists but does not cover all current runtime modules
description: qa:ui:regression passes across 19 modules and 5 viewports, but visual QA fails due to missing weeklyProductionControl in its module list and table regression report still records overflow/warnings.

evidence.note: weeklyProductionControl is missing from visual snapshot module IDs.
Tablet report rows record overflow values for planning/routes and products missing tables.
Regression smoke passes but visual QA fails separately.

impact: A green regression smoke does not prove full visual stability for newly added modules.
recommendedAction: Require runtime module registry and visual snapshot module list to be generated from the same source.

title: Status/badge cleanup is incomplete
description: renderUiStatusToken is used 63 times, but status-pill and local status/badge/chip classes remain in code/CSS.

evidence.note: Shared status token helper exists.
Gantt combines StatusToken with legacy status-pill class names.
Previous report still listed 107 raw local status colors.

impact: Status tone and badge styling can diverge across Gantt, tables, supply, auth, workshop and visual samples.
recommendedAction: Continue status migration but track semantic status state, not just class tokenization.

title: Shell/sidebar/topbar selectors are repeated across many layers
description: Core layout selectors such as .module-tab, .directory-sidebar, .topbar, .module-data-page and .directory-workspace are duplicated across multiple CSS layers.

evidence.note: Shows repeated layout selectors and files.
Primary shell layer.
Older sidebar layout variables coexist with newer shell tokens.

impact: Sidebar/topbar fixes can still create per-module drift, as seen in recent manual QA loops.
recommendedAction: Move app sidebar/topbar/sidebar-item to a single locked CSS family and block page-specific overrides except explicit contracts.

title: Previous pass claims are stale against current command results
description: docs/ui-stabilization-master-plan.md and docs/ui-global-forensic-audit.md claim multiple QA passes, but the current audit run shows failures in those same command families.

evidence.note: Previous report claims qa:ui/css/architecture/functional/git diff pass.
Previous forensic report claims qa:visual and others pass.
Current live run contradicts those claims.

impact: Old docs are useful context but cannot be used as acceptance proof.
recommendedAction: Stamp every future report with current git commit, dirty-state, and machine-readable command output.

title: Legacy tail is still used for new high-specificity fixes
description: The legacy override layer contains authPrototype icon readability rules with many !important declarations and raw rgba/px values.

evidence.note: Recent auth icon fixes live in legacy tail with !important.
Legacy tail has 4019 lines, 344 !important, 1070 raw px.
Previous plan already identified legacy tail as risky.

impact: The compatibility layer is still acting as a patch surface, not only a containment layer.
recommendedAction: Freeze new writes to legacy tail except documented compatibility, and migrate recent auth/sidebar fixes into tokenized module CSS.

title: Typography is not globally controllable
description: CSS contains 963 font-size declarations, 796 raw px font-size declarations, 700 font-weight declarations, and 501 numeric font weights.

evidence.note: Current typography counters.
Local font-size/font-weight values in visualSystem.
Local table header typography with !important.

impact: Changing type scale globally will only partially affect dense screens.
recommendedAction: Create typography budgets by component: topbar/sidebar, table, card, panel, form, gantt.

title: Inline style usage is extensive and partly intentional
description: Audit found 117 style attributes and 19 direct style API calls. Some are necessary for Gantt geometry, but others belong to visual samples and module layouts.

evidence.note: Gantt timeline uses inline geometry.
Gantt slot segments use inline geometry.
Visual samples use inline --level styles.

impact: Inline styles are not all bad, but without classification they weaken token audits and CSS guardrails.
recommendedAction: Classify inline styles as geometry-required, CSS-var-only, demo-only, or debt; fail only unclassified usage.

title: Working tree is too dirty for clean acceptance
description: Current git status contains 90 changed/untracked entries, including docs, reports, scripts, src and styles. git diff --check also fails on trailing whitespace in docs/ui-table-regression-report.md.

evidence.note: Current dirty worktree count and files.
git diff --check failure lines.
Current failure output.

impact: It is hard to distinguish user edits, generated artifacts, and audit changes; acceptance evidence can be contaminated.
recommendedAction: Before major UI refactors, create a clean checkpoint or branch, then keep audit/report output isolated.
